evidence_excerpt: Persistent KFSD skin/hair abnormalities and severe developmental impairment were documented, with no reported resolution or death. At age 6 years, laboratory abnormalities (low cholesterol and apolipoproteins) were noted; ophthalmologic exam showed no eye anomalies. No sustained improvement or deterioration is described.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: He suffered from KFSD with follicular and interfollicular hyperkeratosis on arms, cheeks, and legs, thickened skin of palms/soles, and sparse/thin hair with loss; congenital genitourinary anomalies were present. No indication of symptom resolution.

notes_on_uncertainty: The case report provides limited longitudinal follow-up beyond early childhood; the only long-term data are lipid/apolipoprotein abnormalities at age 6 years. No explicit treatment plan or subsequent clinical changes (improvement/deterioration, complications, re-interventions) are documented, so outcome classification is based on persistence of deficits without evidence of resolution or decline.
